================================================================================
RESIZE, THROTTLING, AND CANVAS SCALE — NOTES FOR LATER
================================================================================
Project: Slider (StateManager). Related code: src/App.jsx, src/canvasScale.js

Last topic: applying “throttle / trailing resize” ideas vs the minified reference
bundles (reference-js-01 … reference-js-05.txt).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. WHAT THE REFERENCE BUNDLE WAS DOING (reference-js-05)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Pattern (minified, ~line 6 in reference-js-05.txt):

  r = function () {
    n();
    t = setTimeout(function () { return n(); }, 160);
  };
  r();
  window.addEventListener("resize", r);

- n() = callback tied to global CSS injection (emotion/styled-style), not your
  canvas math.
- 160 = 160 milliseconds delay for a second pass — NOT 160rem design width.
- Intent-ish: run work immediately on resize, then run again after layout has
  a moment to settle (trailing pass).

This is closer to "immediate + delayed second run" than a strict debounce.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. VOCABULARY (QUICK)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Throttle: During many resize events, runexpensive work at most every X ms (or
  once per frame) — fewer updates, smoother UI.

Trailing: Do the important work at the END of a burst (or after a short delay)
  so sizes match post-layout, not mid-resize glitch.

Debounce: Wait until events pause for Y ms, then run once — different from the
  reference snippet above.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. YOUR APP TODAY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
App.jsx useEffect:
- update() reads getViewportSize() from canvasScale.js (prefers visualViewport).
- setScale(getCanvasContainScale(width, height)).
- Listeners: window "resize", and visualViewport "resize" if present.

canvasScale.js:
- Logical canvas 2560×1440 from 160×90 rem @ 16px root.
- getCanvasContainScale = min(viewportW/CANVAS_W, viewportH/CANVAS_H).

This is already the right class of “fixed artboard, contain scale” logic.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. WHEN TO CHANGE YOUR LISTENERS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
You do NOT need to copy reference-js-05 unless you have a real problem.

Consider rAF throttling if:
- Resize feels janky (too many setState calls while dragging the window).

Consider a short trailing pass (setTimeout 0–150ms or double rAF) if:
- Scale is wrong for one frame after resize, or specific devices glitch.

Consider visualViewport "scroll" if:
- Mobile browser chrome (URL bar) hides/shows and resize alone misses updates.

Optional improvement pattern: schedule update via requestAnimationFrame and
cancel/replace the pending rAF id so at most one run per frame.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. REFERENCE-JS FILES — WHAT THEY WERE GOOD FOR
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
reference-js-01 … 05: Mostly webpack/vendor (React, polyfills, Framer Motion,
Sentry, styled pipeline, bridge code). Searches did not surface a clean
copy-pastable “2560×1440 min scale” implementation. Resize/visualViewport
patterns are still useful as behavioral hints, not as a drop-in formula.

For matching a live site exactly: export the small source chunk that computes
scale (search Sources for innerWidth, visualViewport, 2560, transform, etc.).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6. FILES TO OPEN WHEN REVISITING
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
.slider project:
  src/App.jsx          — listeners + setScale
  src/canvasScale.js   — CANVAS_* , getViewportSize , getCanvasContainScale

Reference dumps (if still present):
  reference-js-05.txt  — resize + setTimeout(..., 160) context

================================================================================
END OF NOTES
================================================================================
